next_steps: Biopsy of the affected orbital tissue or optic nerve sheath
Immunohistochemical staining of biopsy specimen for IgG4 and plasma cell markers
Serum IgG4 subclass quantification (repeat if necessary)
Systemic evaluation for IgG4-related disease involving other organ systems (e.g., pancreas, salivary glands, retroperitoneum)
Consultation with rheumatology and ophthalmology for consideration of systemic corticosteroids or steroid-sparing therapy
Continued monitoring of visual acuity and extraocular motility
Obtain ANA, anti-Ro/SSA, anti-La/SSB, and anti-dsDNA antibodies
Perform a skin biopsy of any new or active rash with direct immunofluorescence
Re-evaluate existing skin biopsy specimens for interface dermatitis or IgG4-positive plasma cells
Perform whole-body PET/CT to evaluate for occult systemic involvement, lymphadenopathy, or malignancy
Consultation with Rheumatology for potential biopsy of the affected orbital tissue or lacrimal gland
Consider repeating serum IgG4 and testing for other autoantibodies (e.g., ENA panel)
